codeReview.summary: Tests cover 9/9 VAL-SETTINGS assertions but 2 tests have insufficient assertions that don't verify the actual behaviors described in the validation contract. VAL-SETTINGS-004 only checks page stability after import, not that data was actually restored. VAL-SETTINGS-005 only checks page stability after invalid import, not that an error message is shown.

issues.description: VAL-SETTINGS-004: Test 'Importing valid export JSON file succeeds and settings page remains stable' only verifies the settings page is still visible after import. It does NOT verify that data was actually restored. The validation contract states 'Importing a valid export JSON file restores all data' but the test creates empty data ({profiles: [], sessions: [], messages: []}) and only checks that the page didn't crash. Should verify that data appears in the UI or via API after import.

VAL-SETTINGS-005: Both tests ('Importing invalid JSON shows error toast' and 'Importing valid JSON but wrong structure shows error') only verify page stability (settings heading visible). Neither test checks for an actual error message/toast. The validation contract states 'shows an error message' but no assertion verifies an error is displayed. Tests should use expect(page.getByText(/error/i)).toBeVisible() or similar.
VAL-SETTINGS-003: Hardcoded year range check (2024-2026) will fail after 2026. Consider using a relative date validation like checking year >= current year instead of a fixed upper bound.

summary: The 18 test cases in e2e/settings-data-comprehensive.spec.ts cover all 9 VAL-SETTINGS assertions (VAL-SETTINGS-001 through VAL-SETTINGS-009). However, 2 tests have blocking issues: VAL-SETTINGS-004 only checks page stability after import, not actual data restoration; VAL-SETTINGS-005 only checks page stability after invalid import, not error message display. The validation contract requires 'data restored' and 'error message shown' respectively, but these are not verified. Tests should be enhanced to check actual functional outcomes, not just absence of crashes.
